company.name: Ionis Pharmaceuticals
company.subtitle: TRYNGOLZA $108M Y1 · Olezarsen sHTG PDUFA Apr 2026 · Donidalorsen launched · RNA platform leader
company.cashRunway: Cash flow breakeven target 2028 (~$2.4B cash + growing revenue)

market.notes: TRYNGOLZA FCS: $108M 2025 revenue (first year). sHTG sNDA PDUFA Apr 3, 2026 — peak sales guided >$2B. Phase 3 CORE/CORE2: 72% TG reduction, 85% reduction in acute pancreatitis. sHTG = ~2M US patients vs ~3K FCS. This is the blockbuster catalyst.

us.patientsK.note: NIH/FCS registry: ~4K US FCS (familial chylomicronemia, LPL deficiency); ~4M sHTG (severe TG >500 mg/dL) per NHANES.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet FCS: ~5K EU5+UK; sHTG ~3M per ESC 2023 European epi.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- Medicare/commercial cover Waylivra (volanesorsen) + emerging plozasiran/olezarsen for FCS; sHTG access tighter.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- EMA approved Waylivra 2019 for FCS; NICE/G-BA gated on pancreatitis history.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% -- Japan NHI FCS coverage emerging; sHTG largely statin/fibrate generics.

us.priceK.note: $300K/yr anchored to Waylivra/olezarsen (Ionis) FCS pricing; sHTG launch 2025-2026 at lower price ~$15K for broader pool.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$180K FCS post HTA; sHTG net ~$8K.

penPct.note: ~25% peak for FCS (small captive market); sHTG penetration capped ~2% given massive pool.

us.reachPct.note: TRYNGOLZA olezarsen FCS approved; sHTG sNDA PDUFA Apr 3 2026 expands addressable

market.notes: First RNA-targeted HAE prophylaxis. $7M Q4 2025 (first full quarter). Early launch momentum — all patient segments. EU launched Jan 2026. Competitors: Takhzyro (lanadelumab), Orladeyo (berotralstat).

us.patientsK.note: US HAE Association: ~7K US HAE diagnosed (~1:50K prevalence); ~20% still undiagnosed per HAEA registry.

eu.patientsK.note: HAE International: ~10K EU27+UK; Germany/France strongest diagnosis rates; C1-INH deficiency type I dominant.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~20K diagnosed; Japan HAE registry ~500 pts; China significantly underdiagnosed.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% -- commercial + Medicare cover Takhzyro/Orladeyo prophylaxis; on-demand Firazyr/Berinert reimbursed.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE approved Takhzyro/Orladeyo; G-BA added benefit; C1-INH (Cinryze/Berinert) first-line prophylaxis.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan PMDA approved lanadelumab; China access limited to on-demand C1-INH.

us.priceK.note: Blended $450K: Takhzyro $600K + Orladeyo $500K + Haegarda $500K + on-demand Firazyr/Berinert $200K per attack.

eu.priceK.note: ~$280K blended -- Takhzyro net ~$350K post-discount; C1-INH prophylaxis $200K.

row.priceK.note: ~$100K -- Japan Takhzyro ~$250K; emerging markets C1-INH on-demand only.

penPct.note: ~70% peak -- prophylaxis shift from on-demand; Donidalorsen/garadacimab pipeline expanding coverage.

us.reachPct.note: DAWNZERA donidalorsen HAE launched Q3 2025; competes with Takhzyro

market.notes: Ultra-rare fatal neurological disease. First disease-modifying therapy. Pivotal data positive — NDA Q1 2026. Small revenue but high-margin orphan. First independent neuro launch for Ionis.

us.patientsK.note: NORD/Orphanet: ~500 US ultra-rare Alexander disease (GFAP mutations); infantile/juvenile/adult forms.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet EU: ~600 Alexander disease EU27+UK; ultra-rare designation.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~3K Alexander disease; Ionis zilganersen (GFAP ASO) in Phase 3.

us.wtpPct.note: ~85% - ultra-rare orphan; Medicaid/commercial cover supportive care; future ASO pricing under orphan designation.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~70% - EMA orphan designation; national funds cover rare pediatric neuro.

row.wtpPct.note: ~30% - Japan covers via PMDA rare disease program; LMICs minimal access.

us.priceK.note: Pipeline ASO $400K+ (Ionis zilganersen benchmark Spinraza $750K Y1); current supportive care <$10K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$250K expected ASO; EU net ~60% of US orphan list.

row.priceK.note: ~$80K; Japan near EU rare disease pricing.

penPct.note: ~60% peak - ultra-rare with concentrated KOL prescribing; diagnosis rate limits beyond genetic testing.

us.reachPct.note: Zilganersen Alexander disease NDA Q1 2026; ultra-rare, first therapy

market.notes: $212M total WAINUA sales 2025 → $49M royalty to Ionis. CARDIO-TTRansform ATTR-CM Phase 3 readout H2 2026 — massive TAM expansion. Partnered with AstraZeneca ($3.6B deal). If ATTR-CM positive: $5B+ peak sales potential.

us.patientsK.note: Alnylam 10-K 2024: ~10K US hATTR-PN (TTR mutation carriers with polyneuropathy), driven by Onpattro/Amvuttra uptake post-APOLLO.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet/Alnylam EU: ~10K hATTR-PN across EU5 (Portugal V30M cluster, Sweden, Cyprus endemic regions).

row.patientsK.note: Japan V30M endemic ~1K; Brazil/AR Portuguese-descent clusters; global ROW ~8K hATTR-PN.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% -- Medicare Part B (Onpattro infusion) and Part D (Amvuttra SC) both covered post-APOLLO/HELIOS-A outcomes data.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- NICE/HAS reimburse patisiran/vutrisiran for Stage 1-2 hATTR-PN with polyneuropathy FAP confirmation.

row.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Japan NHI covers patisiran since 2019; Brazil SUS limited access via judicial orders.

us.priceK.note: Amvuttra $476K/yr WAC (Alnylam), Onpattro $450K; blended $460K reflects SC shift.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$280K post HTA; UK NICE $250K, Germany AMNOG ~$300K.

row.priceK.note: $180K blended: Japan NHI ~$250K, Brazil judicial ~$150K, tiered emerging ~$80K.

penPct.note: ~55% peak -- mature ASO/siRNA competition (Wainua, Amvuttra) saturates diagnosed pool.

us.reachPct.note: WAINUA eplontersen ATTRv-PN + Phase 3 CARDIO-TTRansform ATTR-CM; AZ partnered

assets.name: Bepirovirsen — chronic Hep B (partnered w/ GSK)

market.notes: B-Well 1+2 Phase 3 positive — first functional cure for CHB. Partnered with GSK. ~300M people with CHB globally. If approved: milestone + royalty stream.

us.patientsK.note: CDC 2023 + WHO: ~2.4M US chronic HBV (HBsAg+); ~800K on tenofovir/entecavir NUCs, functional cure candidates ~500K.

eu.patientsK.note: ECDC 2023: ~4.7M EU chronic HBV; ~1.5M on NUCs eligible for siRNA/pegIFN combination functional cure regimens.

row.patientsK.note: WHO global: ~296M chronic HBV (Asia-Pac dominant); China 75M, Africa 80M; treatable pool ~50M.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- Medicare/commercial cover NUCs; functional cure regimens (GSK bepirovirsen, VIR-2218) will require strict HBsAg/DNA gates.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- EASL guidelines + G-BA/NICE reimburse NUCs; cure combos gated on Ph3 outcomes 2026+.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- China NRDL generic NUCs dominant; novel functional cure access limited near-term.

us.priceK.note: $15K/yr blended anchored to NUC generics ($200) + emerging cure regimens Ph3 (GSK bepirovirsen ASO, Arbutus siRNA pricing TBD $40-60K).

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$8K: NUC generics dominant, functional cure launch 2027+ at ~$30K.

penPct.note: ~4% peak -- functional cure Ph3 gated 2026-2027; NUCs mature + generic.

us.reachPct.note: Bepirovirsen HBV B-Well Phase 3 positive; first functional cure candidate

market.notes: HORIZON Phase 3 CVOT readout mid-2026. Partnered with Novartis. >20% of population has elevated Lp(a). If positive: first Lp(a)-lowering therapy with outcomes data. Multi-billion peak sales for Novartis → milestones + royalties to Ionis.

us.patientsK.note: AHA Lp(a) Consensus 2024: ~64M US Lp(a) >=50 mg/dL; treated pool narrower ~100K eventual (olpasiran, pelacarsen, lepodisiran Ph3).

eu.patientsK.note: EAS 2022: ~50M EU with elevated Lp(a); ~60K treatment-eligible at launch given HTA gates.

row.patientsK.note: Global ~700M elevated Lp(a); Japan/AU early adopters, treatable pool ~80K.

us.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- Medicare/commercial will require prior ASCVD + LDL optimized; Lp(a) screening still limited to ~10% of population.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- NICE/G-BA will tightly gate Lp(a) siRNA/ASO to secondary prevention + Lp(a) >=70 mg/dL.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan early adoption; emerging markets limited Lp(a) testing infrastructure.

us.priceK.note: $8K/yr anchored to Novartis pelacarsen / Amgen olpasiran / Lilly lepodisiran Ph3 pricing analog (similar to PCSK9i premium).

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$4K post HTA negotiation for Lp(a)-specific therapy launch 2026-2027.

row.priceK.note: $2K blended -- Japan NHI likely leads ex-US/EU adoption.

penPct.note: ~3% peak reflects diagnosis bottleneck + HTA gates; HORIZON / OCEAN(a) Ph3 CV outcomes 2025-2026 gate growth.

us.reachPct.note: Pelacarsen Lp(a) Phase 3 HORIZON CVOT readout mid-2026 (Novartis partnered)

mega_bear.narrative: Olezarsen sHTG rejected + ATTR-CM fails + Lp(a) misses CVOT
events: ✗ FDA rejects olezarsen sHTG — safety concern or insufficient data
✗ CARDIO-TTRansform ATTR-CM misses — AstraZeneca deal devalued
✗ HORIZON Lp(a) CVOT misses — Novartis partnership gutted
→ Revenue growth stalls, cash burn accelerates, stock to $15-20

bear.narrative: Olezarsen sHTG approved but slow uptake + partner readouts mixed
events: ✓ Olezarsen sHTG approved but payer pushback on pricing
~ ATTR-CM marginal — benefit real but modest vs Alnylam
✗ Lp(a) HORIZON borderline — no clear CV outcome benefit
→ Revenue grows but path to breakeven pushes to 2029+

base.narrative: Olezarsen sHTG blockbuster launch + ATTR-CM positive + Lp(a) shows signal
events: ✓ Olezarsen sHTG approved Apr 2026 — rapid uptake, >$2B peak guided
✓ CARDIO-TTRansform positive — ATTR-CM label for eplontersen
~ HORIZON Lp(a) shows trend but formal CVOT benefit borderline
✓ Bepirovirsen CHB filed by GSK — milestones flowing

bull.narrative: Multi-blockbuster RNA platform — TG + ATTR-CM + Lp(a) all hit
events: ✓ Olezarsen becomes $3B+ franchise (sHTG + broader TG)
✓ ATTR-CM blockbuster — eplontersen $5B+ for AstraZeneca
✓ Lp(a) HORIZON positive — Novartis $10B+ franchise
✓ Platform generates $1B+ in royalties by 2028

psychedelic_bull.narrative: RNA therapeutics leader — Ionis becomes the Genentech of antisense — AZ/Novartis acquires
events: ✓ TG + ATTR-CM + Lp(a) + CHB — four blockbuster franchises simultaneously
✓ ION775 (siRNA) extends platform into next-gen modalities
✓ Neurology pipeline (Angelman, SMA, Huntington's) generates 3+ more drugs
✓ AstraZeneca or Novartis acquires entire platform at $100+/share

lastData.title: TRYNGOLZA $108M Y1 + olezarsen sHTG peak guided >$2B
points: Olezarsen sHTG: sNDA submitted, PDUFA Apr 3, 2026
DAWNZERA (donidalorsen HAE): $7M Q4, first full quarter, EU approved
WAINUA (eplontersen): $212M total 2025 sales → $49M royalty to Ionis
Bepirovirsen CHB: B-Well 1+2 Phase 3 positive — first functional cure
Zilganersen Alexander disease: NDA submitted Q1 2026

nextCatalyst.title: Olezarsen sHTG PDUFA — biggest near-term catalyst
thresholds: sHTG = ~2M US patients (vs 3K FCS) — 600x TAM expansion
72% TG reduction + 85% reduction in acute pancreatitis in Phase 3
Also mid-2026: HORIZON Lp(a) CVOT readout (Novartis)

risks: Olezarsen sHTG pricing/reimbursement risk — payer pushback in large population
HORIZON Lp(a) CVOT is high-risk — Lp(a) lowering may not translate to CV outcomes
ATTR-CM competitive with Alnylam's vutrisiran (HELIOS-B positive)
Cash burn ~$500M/yr — breakeven 2028 target depends on sHTG launch success
Complex business model — mix of own products, co-commercialization, royalties
RNA therapeutics face delivery challenges in some tissues
Ionis has historically overpromised on pipeline timelines
Stock underperformed peers despite strong pipeline — market skepticism on execution

catalysts.title: TRYNGOLZA sHTG sNDA FDA approval (PDUFA)
catalysts._rationale: FDA set official PDUFA date of June 30, 2026 for olezarsen sHTG indication with priority review

catalysts.title: CARDIO-TTRansform ATTR-CM Phase 3 data readout
catalysts._rationale: Confirmed H2 2026 readout for CARDIO-TTRansform trial in ATTR cardiomyopathy

catalysts.title: Zilganersen Alexander disease NDA FDA approval (PDUFA)
catalysts._rationale: FDA accepted NDA with Priority Review and set PDUFA date for September 22, 2026
